qm_experiments.title: Born/context probability tests
qm_experiments.standard_result: Context probabilities are nonnegative and normalized by the selected readout context.

idt_primitives.event: Resolved alternatives A_i are event bundles in the selected context.
idt_primitives.distinguishability: Off-diagonal actualization terms must remain below the context admissibility threshold.
idt_primitives.inheritance: The inherited kernel supplies the actualization measure mu(A_i).
idt_primitives.readout_context: The context K selects the finite partition whose weights are normalized.
idt_primitives.facticity: A factual outcome is one resolved cell of the context partition.
idt_primitives.stable_invariant: The normalized probability table is stable under the declared finite context.

qm_experiments.claim_boundary: Finite Born readout is executable; global Born postulate and full QM are not claimed.

qm_experiments.title: Two-path interference
qm_experiments.standard_result: Balanced two-path readout gives P(phi)=1/2(1+V cos phi).

idt_primitives.event: The two path alternatives are the event bundles h0 and h1.
idt_primitives.distinguishability: Incomplete path distinguishability is encoded by Gamma_I(h0,h1).
idt_primitives.inheritance: The phase-shifted inheritance act changes the relative history weight.
idt_primitives.readout_context: The output port context compares phi with the complementary phi+pi output.
idt_primitives.facticity: A detector click facticizes one output event.
idt_primitives.stable_invariant: Visibility is fixed by the inherited coherence magnitude before readout.

qm_experiments.claim_boundary: Finite fringe shape is executable; propagation phase geometry is still open.

qm_experiments.title: Finite I3 actualization
qm_experiments.standard_result: Bilinear quantum readout has no primitive third-order interference term.

idt_primitives.event: Three disjoint alternatives A, B, and C define the tested event bundles.
idt_primitives.distinguishability: Pairwise distinguishability enters through the inherited bilinear kernel.
idt_primitives.inheritance: The same inherited actualization law is applied to singles, pairs, and triples.
idt_primitives.readout_context: The slit/readout context selects which unions are compared.
idt_primitives.facticity: Each run facticizes a detector event after the selected slit context.
idt_primitives.stable_invariant: The third-order inclusion-exclusion residual vanishes.

qm_experiments.claim_boundary: Bilinear finite actualization is checked; no beyond-QM residual is claimed.

qm_experiments.title: Triple-slit Sorkin parameter
qm_experiments.standard_result: The normalized Sorkin parameter remains below the declared tolerance.

idt_primitives.event: The three slit alternatives and their unions are event bundles.
idt_primitives.distinguishability: Only pairwise inherited distinguishability contributes to the measure.
idt_primitives.inheritance: The same actualization measure is reused across all slit combinations.
idt_primitives.readout_context: The experiment cycles through compatible slit-open contexts.
idt_primitives.facticity: Detector impact events are compared across contexts after normalization.
idt_primitives.stable_invariant: The normalized third-order residual kappa_I3 is bounded.

qm_experiments.claim_boundary: Known triple-slit compatibility is executable; no new residual prediction is claimed.

qm_experiments.title: Which-way marker visibility loss
qm_experiments.standard_result: Visibility decreases as which-way distinguishability increases.

idt_primitives.event: The path alternatives are coupled to marker alternatives.
idt_primitives.distinguishability: Marker overlap controls how distinguishable the paths become.
idt_primitives.inheritance: The marker channel inherits path distinctions into the apparatus sector.
idt_primitives.readout_context: The interference readout context ignores or resolves the marker sector.
idt_primitives.facticity: A path-marking record can become a stable fact in the marker context.
idt_primitives.stable_invariant: Visibility and distinguishability obey the finite complementarity relation.

qm_experiments.claim_boundary: Finite marker visibility is executable; full environment dynamics is not derived.

qm_experiments.title: Quantum eraser
qm_experiments.standard_result: Conditioning on an eraser context can recover interference visibility.

idt_primitives.event: Path-marker alternatives remain available before the conditioned readout.
idt_primitives.distinguishability: Effective path distinguishability depends on the conditioned marker overlap.
idt_primitives.inheritance: A later conditioning context changes which inherited distinctions are read out.
idt_primitives.readout_context: The eraser context reads marker superpositions rather than which-way records.
idt_primitives.facticity: Factual coincidence subensembles are selected by the conditioned context.
idt_primitives.stable_invariant: Recovered visibility is fixed by the conditioned overlap.

qm_experiments.claim_boundary: Conditional eraser readout is executable; retrocausal claims are not made.

qm_experiments.title: Unitary measurement context
qm_experiments.standard_result: Changing measurement basis changes the readout table by a unitary context map.

idt_primitives.event: Resolved basis outcomes are context-indexed event bundles.
idt_primitives.distinguishability: The context basis selects which alternatives are mutually distinguishable.
idt_primitives.inheritance: The unitary context map carries the amplitude packet into the readout basis.
idt_primitives.readout_context: U_K is the finite admissible measurement context.
idt_primitives.facticity: A detector outcome facticizes one component of the context packet.
idt_primitives.stable_invariant: The context probability table is |U_K psi|^2.

qm_experiments.claim_boundary: Finite context readout is executable; apparatus derivation of U_K is open.

qm_experiments.title: Finite interferometer network
qm_experiments.standard_result: A sequence of unitary components produces the final output probability table.

idt_primitives.event: Intermediate and final ports are event bundles in a network context.
idt_primitives.distinguishability: Coherent path alternatives remain partially indistinguishable through the network.
idt_primitives.inheritance: Each component is an admissible update/context map in the sequence.
idt_primitives.readout_context: The final output basis selects the factual detector partition.
idt_primitives.facticity: Only the final detector click is facticized in the finite network gate.
idt_primitives.stable_invariant: The output probabilities are stable under the declared unitary product.

qm_experiments.claim_boundary: Finite network readout is executable; continuum propagation is not derived here.

qm_experiments.title: Projective measurement repeatability
qm_experiments.standard_result: Repeating the same ideal projective measurement returns the same nonzero outcome.

idt_primitives.event: Projector ranges are the event bundles of the measurement context.
idt_primitives.distinguishability: Projectors define mutually distinguishable factual alternatives.
idt_primitives.inheritance: Projection updates the packet into the selected factual event.
idt_primitives.readout_context: The same projective context is applied before and after facticity.
idt_primitives.facticity: The first nonzero outcome becomes stable under repeated same-context readout.
idt_primitives.stable_invariant: The post-readout state has unit probability for the same projector.

qm_experiments.claim_boundary: Ideal repeatability is executable; irreversible collapse dynamics is not derived.

qm_experiments.title: Bell/CHSH probability table
qm_experiments.standard_result: Context-indexed joint tables obey no-signalling and the Tsirelson-compatible bound.

idt_primitives.event: Joint outcomes (a,b|x,y) are event bundles indexed by settings.
idt_primitives.distinguishability: Each local setting context defines which outcomes are distinguishable.
idt_primitives.inheritance: The correlation structure is inherited across the bipartite preparation.
idt_primitives.readout_context: No single global factual context over all settings is assumed.
idt_primitives.facticity: Each run facticizes only one setting-indexed joint outcome.
idt_primitives.stable_invariant: No-signalling marginals and CHSH value are context-stable.

qm_experiments.claim_boundary: Finite Bell table validation is executable; Bell probabilities are not fully derived from primitives.

qm_experiments.title: Bell/CHSH from amplitudes
qm_experiments.standard_result: Joint probability tables are computed from amplitude tables before CHSH validation.

idt_primitives.event: Bipartite outcome alternatives are encoded as amplitude components.
idt_primitives.distinguishability: Outcome distinguishability is selected separately for each setting context.
idt_primitives.inheritance: Amplitude packets inherit the preparation correlation into setting-indexed readouts.
idt_primitives.readout_context: Each setting pair supplies a finite amplitude readout context.
idt_primitives.facticity: Only one joint result becomes factual per selected setting pair.
idt_primitives.stable_invariant: The amplitude-derived table satisfies no-signalling and CHSH bounds.

qm_experiments.claim_boundary: Amplitude-to-table bridge is executable; primitive local apparatus dynamics remains open.

qm_experiments.title: Singlet angle Bell model
qm_experiments.standard_result: The singlet correlation is E(alpha,beta)=-cos(alpha-beta) and reaches |S|=2 sqrt(2).

idt_primitives.event: Binary spin outcomes are event bundles indexed by two apparatus angles.
idt_primitives.distinguishability: The angle pair selects the distinguishability partition for the joint readout.
idt_primitives.inheritance: The singlet preparation inherits anti-correlated structure into all allowed contexts.
idt_primitives.readout_context: The selected analyzer angles define the finite Bell context.
idt_primitives.facticity: Each detector pair facticizes one context-indexed joint outcome.
idt_primitives.stable_invariant: The angle correlation table gives the Tsirelson value for the selected settings.

qm_experiments.claim_boundary: Finite singlet angle readout is executable; spin representation theory is not derived.

qm_experiments.title: Calibrated action-phase holdout
qm_experiments.standard_result: One action-phase anchor supports E=hbar omega, p=hbar k, and phase=S/hbar holdouts.

idt_primitives.event: Frequency, momentum, and phase readouts are separate experimental event families.
idt_primitives.distinguishability: Phase and generator distinctions are compared through one calibrated action scale.
idt_primitives.inheritance: The calibrated action anchor is inherited across multiple QM readout routes.
idt_primitives.readout_context: Each route supplies a different operational context for the same anchor.
idt_primitives.facticity: Measured spectral, momentum, or phase outcomes are factual readouts in their domains.
idt_primitives.stable_invariant: The same calibrated hbar is used without per-domain refit.

qm_experiments.claim_boundary: Calibrated consistency is executable; first-principles hbar_I remains blocked.

qm_experiments.title: Decoherence and recoverability loss
qm_experiments.standard_result: Environment marking suppresses off-diagonal coherence, and recoverability loss separates erasure from stable facticity.

idt_primitives.event: System and environment alternatives form the joint event bundle.
idt_primitives.distinguishability: Environmental records increase distinguishability between system alternatives.
idt_primitives.inheritance: Distinguishability is inherited into the environment through premeasurement coupling.
idt_primitives.readout_context: Pointer/environment contexts select whether records are recoverable or factual.
idt_primitives.facticity: A large recoverability loss marks stable facticity rather than reversible marking.
idt_primitives.stable_invariant: Residual coherence and recoverability loss meet the declared thresholds.

qm_experiments.stable_invariant: offdiagonal coherence <= threshold and Lambda_irrev >= threshold
qm_experiments.claim_boundary: Finite decoherence criteria are executable; complete macroscopic apparatus construction is open.

qm_experiments.title: Stern-Gerlach single-axis readout
qm_experiments.standard_result: A spin-1/2 packet yields two outcomes in the selected magnet-axis context.

idt_primitives.event: The up/down beam packets are event bundles in the chosen axis context.
idt_primitives.distinguishability: Spatial separation makes the two axis outcomes distinguishable.
idt_primitives.inheritance: The magnet apparatus inherits spin-context distinction into path separation.
idt_primitives.readout_context: The magnet orientation selects the admissible spin readout context.
idt_primitives.facticity: A detector hit facticizes one branch in that axis context.
idt_primitives.stable_invariant: Outcome probabilities are stable for repeated preparation in the same context.

qm_experiments.stable_invariant: same-axis prepared eigenstate gives repeatable outcome
qm_experiments.claim_boundary: Finite single-axis SG context readout is executable; physical magnet apparatus derivation is open.

qm_experiments.title: Sequential Stern-Gerlach contexts
qm_experiments.standard_result: SG_z+ followed by SG_z is repeatable, but SG_z+ then SG_x then SG_z returns half-half z outcomes.

idt_primitives.event: Successive axis outcomes are event bundles indexed by measurement order.
idt_primitives.distinguishability: Each axis defines a different distinguishability partition.
idt_primitives.inheritance: The intermediate x-context updates which distinctions remain inherited into the final z-context.
idt_primitives.readout_context: The ordered SG_z, SG_x, SG_z apparatus sequence defines the tested contexts.
idt_primitives.facticity: A fact is stable only relative to compatible repeated contexts.
idt_primitives.stable_invariant: Inserting an incompatible context destroys same-axis z repeatability.

qm_experiments.claim_boundary: Finite sequential SG context reset is executable; primitive apparatus construction remains open.

qm_experiments.title: Delayed-choice interferometry
qm_experiments.standard_result: The late apparatus choice selects particle-like or wave-like readout statistics without retrocausal signalling.

idt_primitives.event: Open-path and recombined-path alternatives remain possible before final readout.
idt_primitives.distinguishability: The final context decides whether path distinctions are resolved or erased.
idt_primitives.inheritance: The late context changes inherited readout compatibility, not past event existence.
idt_primitives.readout_context: The final beam-splitter choice selects the factual partition.
idt_primitives.facticity: Only the final detector context facticizes the outcome.
idt_primitives.stable_invariant: Context-indexed statistics match particle or interference tables without signalling.

qm_experiments.stable_invariant: late context changes readout table, not past facts
qm_experiments.claim_boundary: Finite delayed-context readout is executable; primitive apparatus timing derivation remains open.

qm_experiments.title: Aharonov-Bohm phase
qm_experiments.standard_result: A closed electromagnetic holonomy shifts interference phase even where local force vanishes along the paths.

idt_primitives.event: The charged two-path alternatives enclose a flux region.
idt_primitives.distinguishability: The paths remain locally indistinguishable while their relative phase changes.
idt_primitives.inheritance: The phase holonomy is inherited as a nonlocal relation between path alternatives.
idt_primitives.readout_context: The recombination context reads out the phase shift as fringe displacement.
idt_primitives.facticity: Detector events facticize the shifted interference pattern.
idt_primitives.stable_invariant: The closed holonomy phase is gauge-invariant modulo 2pi.

qm_experiments.claim_boundary: Finite AB holonomy phase readout is executable; primitive charge and hbar derivations remain open.

qm_experiments.title: Aharonov-Bohm flux period
qm_experiments.standard_result: The single-charge two-path phase is periodic with Phi_0=h/|q|.

idt_primitives.event: Fringe maxima and minima are event families over changing flux.
idt_primitives.distinguishability: Flux changes phase distinguishability without local path-force readout.
idt_primitives.inheritance: The charge/action bridge inherits phase periodicity into the two-path packet.
idt_primitives.readout_context: The interferometer context reads flux changes as periodic fringe shifts.
idt_primitives.facticity: Each detector impact samples the periodic phase context.
idt_primitives.stable_invariant: The flux period is stable for the declared charge sector.

qm_experiments.claim_boundary: Finite flux-period closure is executable; charge-scale derivation is still open.

qm_experiments.title: Ramsey interferometry
qm_experiments.standard_result: Two separated pulses produce phase-dependent population fringes.

idt_primitives.event: Internal clock-state alternatives form the two Ramsey histories.
idt_primitives.distinguishability: Relative phase controls distinguishability at final recombination.
idt_primitives.inheritance: Free evolution inherits clock phase between the two pulse contexts.
idt_primitives.readout_context: The second pulse selects the final population readout basis.
idt_primitives.facticity: Population measurement facticizes one internal-state outcome.
idt_primitives.stable_invariant: Fringe phase is stable under the declared clock evolution.

qm_experiments.stable_invariant: population fringe depends on accumulated phase
qm_experiments.claim_boundary: Finite Ramsey phase readout is executable; primitive clock/apparatus construction remains open.

qm_experiments.title: Rabi oscillation
qm_experiments.standard_result: A driven two-level system transfers population sinusoidally between outcomes.

idt_primitives.event: The two level outcomes are event bundles of the driven context.
idt_primitives.distinguishability: Drive phase and detuning control distinguishability between level histories.
idt_primitives.inheritance: The drive inheritance act rotates amplitude weight between outcomes.
idt_primitives.readout_context: Population readout selects the level basis after a drive time.
idt_primitives.facticity: A level detection facticizes one outcome after the drive.
idt_primitives.stable_invariant: The oscillation frequency and population table are fixed by the declared update.

qm_experiments.stable_invariant: two-level population oscillation under fixed drive
qm_experiments.claim_boundary: Finite resonant two-level update is executable; microscopic drive derivation is not claimed.

qm_experiments.title: Photoelectric threshold
qm_experiments.standard_result: Emission occurs only when photon energy exceeds the material threshold.

idt_primitives.event: Emission and no-emission are the factual event alternatives.
idt_primitives.distinguishability: Above threshold, emission becomes distinguishable from bound-state continuation.
idt_primitives.inheritance: The action-frequency anchor transfers into the material excitation context.
idt_primitives.readout_context: The detector/work-function context selects emission as the readout.
idt_primitives.facticity: An emitted electron is the facticized threshold-crossing event.
idt_primitives.stable_invariant: The threshold is tied to one calibrated action-frequency anchor.

qm_experiments.stable_invariant: emission threshold follows h f >= W
qm_experiments.claim_boundary: Finite threshold readout is executable with a declared action anchor; material response derivation is not claimed.

qm_experiments.title: Spectroscopy lines
qm_experiments.standard_result: Stable transition frequencies match energy differences through one action-frequency anchor.

idt_primitives.event: Initial and final atomic states plus emitted photon are event bundles.
idt_primitives.distinguishability: Discrete spectral lines are distinguishable transition readouts.
idt_primitives.inheritance: Transition structure inherits stable level differences into frequency readouts.
idt_primitives.readout_context: The spectrometer context selects line frequency as the factual output.
idt_primitives.facticity: Photon detection facticizes one transition event.
idt_primitives.stable_invariant: One calibrated hbar maps all declared transition frequencies.

qm_experiments.stable_invariant: Delta E = h f across spectral lines without per-line refit
qm_experiments.claim_boundary: Finite shared-anchor spectral consistency is executable; atomic level derivation is not claimed.

qm_experiments.title: Tunneling through a barrier
qm_experiments.standard_result: A classically blocked region can have nonzero transmission amplitude.

idt_primitives.event: Reflection and transmission are event bundles after the barrier context.
idt_primitives.distinguishability: Barrier structure changes distinguishability between transmitted and reflected histories.
idt_primitives.inheritance: Suppressed inherited weight remains admissible through the barrier alternative.
idt_primitives.readout_context: Detectors on each side select the reflection/transmission readout.
idt_primitives.facticity: A detector click facticizes either reflected or transmitted outcome.
idt_primitives.stable_invariant: Transmission probability is fixed by the declared barrier update.

qm_experiments.stable_invariant: nonzero transmission for finite barrier with suppressed weight
qm_experiments.claim_boundary: Finite suppressed-transmission readout is executable; exact material barrier dynamics are not claimed.

qm_experiments.title: Quantum Zeno effect
qm_experiments.standard_result: Frequent compatible measurements inhibit transition away from the initial state.

idt_primitives.event: Initial-state survival and transition are event alternatives over time.
idt_primitives.distinguishability: Repeated readout keeps the initial-state distinction factically refreshed.
idt_primitives.inheritance: Frequent context acts interrupt the update that would transfer weight.
idt_primitives.readout_context: The same survival context is applied repeatedly.
idt_primitives.facticity: Each readout re-facticizes survival or transition.
idt_primitives.stable_invariant: Survival probability approaches one as context interval shrinks.

qm_experiments.claim_boundary: Finite repeated-context survival is executable; continuous measurement model is open.

qm_experiments.title: Hong-Ou-Mandel interference
qm_experiments.standard_result: Indistinguishable photons entering a beamsplitter suppress coincidence counts.

idt_primitives.event: Coincidence and bunching outputs are two-particle event bundles.
idt_primitives.distinguishability: Coincidence suppression occurs when particle alternatives become indistinguishable.
idt_primitives.inheritance: The beamsplitter inherits exchange symmetry into the two-particle readout.
idt_primitives.readout_context: The output-port coincidence context selects the tested partition.
idt_primitives.facticity: Detector coincidences or bunching events are facticized outcomes.
idt_primitives.stable_invariant: Coincidence probability vanishes for identical synchronized inputs.

qm_experiments.claim_boundary: Finite overlap-to-coincidence readout is executable; full photonic source/apparatus derivation is not claimed.

qm_experiments.title: Antibunching and single-photon source tests
qm_experiments.standard_result: A single quantum source suppresses simultaneous double detection.

idt_primitives.event: Single and double detection outcomes are source-context event bundles.
idt_primitives.distinguishability: One factual emission excludes a second simultaneous emission in the same source context.
idt_primitives.inheritance: Source preparation inherits single-quantum constraint into detection events.
idt_primitives.readout_context: Coincidence counting selects the single-vs-double event partition.
idt_primitives.facticity: Detection facticizes the emitted quantum count.
idt_primitives.stable_invariant: The zero-delay second-order correlation is below the classical bound.

qm_experiments.stable_invariant: g2(0) < 1 with ideal single-photon limit g2(0)=0
qm_experiments.claim_boundary: Finite coincidence-count facticity gate is executable; source physics is not derived.

qm_experiments.title: Entanglement swapping
qm_experiments.standard_result: A joint Bell readout can condition correlations between systems that did not directly interact.

idt_primitives.event: Pair outcomes and Bell-readout outcomes are event bundles across subsystems.
idt_primitives.distinguishability: The Bell context erases some pair distinctions while selecting joint correlations.
idt_primitives.inheritance: Conditional readout transfers inherited correlation structure between remote alternatives.
idt_primitives.readout_context: The Bell-state measurement selects the conditioning context.
idt_primitives.facticity: The Bell outcome facticizes the subensemble in which remote correlations are read.
idt_primitives.stable_invariant: Conditioned remote correlation table matches the selected Bell outcome.

qm_experiments.stable_invariant: conditional Bell readout swaps correlation structure
qm_experiments.claim_boundary: Finite Bell-outcome correlation transfer is executable; full source/network derivation is not claimed.

qm_experiments.title: Quantum teleportation
qm_experiments.standard_result: An unknown input state is reconstructed at a target after Bell readout and classical correction, without cloning.

idt_primitives.event: Input, Bell-measurement, classical-message, and target outcomes are event bundles.
idt_primitives.distinguishability: The unknown packet is not made globally distinguishable by the protocol.
idt_primitives.inheritance: Entanglement plus Bell facticity transfers the readout structure to the target context.
idt_primitives.readout_context: Bell readout and correction select the target reconstruction context.
idt_primitives.facticity: The Bell outcome and classical message facticize the required correction branch.
idt_primitives.stable_invariant: The target readout reproduces the input context statistics without copying.

qm_experiments.stable_invariant: context transfer plus correction preserves target readout statistics
qm_experiments.claim_boundary: Finite Bell-branch correction is executable; full physical protocol derivation is not claimed.

qm_experiments.title: No-cloning
qm_experiments.standard_result: No universal operation copies an arbitrary unknown quantum state while preserving all readout statistics.

idt_primitives.event: Original and would-be copy readouts are event bundles across future contexts.
idt_primitives.distinguishability: Unknown alternatives cannot be made distinguishable enough to duplicate all contexts.
idt_primitives.inheritance: A universal copy act would need to preserve inherited relations for every possible packet.
idt_primitives.readout_context: The obstruction appears when all future readout contexts are required.
idt_primitives.facticity: Copy verification would facticize incompatible context statistics at once.
idt_primitives.stable_invariant: Inner products/readout overlaps cannot be preserved by universal duplication.

qm_experiments.stable_invariant: unknown packet context statistics cannot be universally cloned
qm_experiments.claim_boundary: Finite inner-product obstruction is executable; no physical cloning machine model is claimed.

qm_experiments.title: GHZ/Mermin contextuality
qm_experiments.standard_result: Multipartite deterministic context products contradict a global pre-existing value assignment.

idt_primitives.event: Multipartite outcome products are event bundles indexed by joint contexts.
idt_primitives.distinguishability: Each commuting context supplies a distinguishable product partition.
idt_primitives.inheritance: The shared preparation inherits constraints across incompatible context families.
idt_primitives.readout_context: Only one compatible product context is read out per run.
idt_primitives.facticity: Product outcomes are factual only inside their selected context.
idt_primitives.stable_invariant: The context product constraints obstruct a global factual assignment.

qm_experiments.stable_invariant: Mermin/GHZ product contradiction for global values
qm_experiments.claim_boundary: Finite Mermin parity obstruction is executable; full multipartite apparatus derivation is not claimed.

qm_experiments.title: Kochen-Specker contextuality
qm_experiments.standard_result: No noncontextual value assignment satisfies all compatible measurement constraints.

idt_primitives.event: Projector outcomes are event bundles grouped into compatible contexts.
idt_primitives.distinguishability: Each context defines a local distinguishability partition.
idt_primitives.inheritance: The same projector may inherit constraints differently across context embeddings.
idt_primitives.readout_context: Factual assignments are only admissible inside selected compatible contexts.
idt_primitives.facticity: A global fact table across all contexts is obstructed.
idt_primitives.stable_invariant: The finite context hypergraph has no valid global assignment.

qm_experiments.claim_boundary: Finite parity-hypergraph obstruction is executable; physical projector-set derivation is not claimed.

qm_experiments.title: Leggett-Garg temporal facticity
qm_experiments.standard_result: Temporal correlation tests can violate macrorealist noninvasive global assignment bounds.

idt_primitives.event: Time-indexed outcomes are event bundles in a temporal readout sequence.
idt_primitives.distinguishability: Each time readout changes which future alternatives remain distinguishable.
idt_primitives.inheritance: Temporal inheritance carries update and disturbance between readout contexts.
idt_primitives.readout_context: Two-time and three-time measurement contexts are not automatically globally compatible.
idt_primitives.facticity: Macroscopic facticity over time is tested as a global assignment claim.
idt_primitives.stable_invariant: Temporal correlations violate the declared macro-facticity bound when contexts are incompatible.

qm_experiments.stable_invariant: Leggett-Garg inequality violation as temporal context obstruction
qm_experiments.claim_boundary: Finite temporal-correlation bound violation is executable; no macrorealist residual model is claimed.

qm_experiments.title: Weak measurement
qm_experiments.standard_result: Partial readout extracts limited information while disturbing the packet less than a projective readout.

idt_primitives.event: Pointer shifts and postselected outcomes are event bundles.
idt_primitives.distinguishability: Weak coupling increases distinguishability only partially.
idt_primitives.inheritance: The apparatus inherits a small amount of system distinction into the pointer.
idt_primitives.readout_context: Preselection, weak pointer readout, and postselection define the context chain.
idt_primitives.facticity: Pointer and postselection events are factual while the intermediate system fact remains partial.
idt_primitives.stable_invariant: Weak value/readout shift is stable for the declared weak-coupling context.

qm_experiments.claim_boundary: Finite weak pointer-shift and partial-facticity gate is executable; full weak-measurement apparatus derivation is open.

qm_experiments.title: Quantum random walk
qm_experiments.standard_result: Unitary graph propagation produces interference-pattern distributions unlike a classical random walk.

idt_primitives.event: Graph-position and coin outcomes are event bundles.
idt_primitives.distinguishability: Path alternatives through the graph remain coherently distinguishable only at readout.
idt_primitives.inheritance: Coin and shift acts inherit amplitude through the graph sequence.
idt_primitives.readout_context: Final graph-position measurement selects the factual distribution.
idt_primitives.facticity: A final position detection facticizes one path-summed outcome.
idt_primitives.stable_invariant: The final distribution follows the declared unitary graph update.

qm_experiments.stable_invariant: unitary graph-walk distribution from coherent path inheritance
qm_experiments.claim_boundary: Finite Hadamard graph-walk distribution is executable; general graph-walk theory is not claimed.

qm_universal_patterns.title: Contextual probability and facticized readout
qm_universal_patterns.mechanism: A prepared event packet is evaluated only after an explicit readout context selects the distinguishability partition.

operations.event_packet: prepare finite alternatives or pointer shifts before final readout
operations.distinguishability_partition: declare the partition made distinguishable by the selected context
operations.inheritance_update: propagate amplitudes or partial pointer information into that context
operations.readout_context: compute the probability table in the chosen basis or partial pointer context
operations.facticity_rule: mark only the selected context outcome as factual, with repeatability or partiality declared
operations.stable_invariant: verify the normalized probability, repeatability, or partial-readout table

qm_universal_patterns.claim_boundary: This pattern unifies finite contextual readouts; it does not derive all apparatus contexts.

qm_universal_patterns.title: Coherent alternatives and interference readout
qm_universal_patterns.mechanism: Unfacticized alternatives inherit phase/coherence through updates and become visible as interference only at readout.

operations.event_packet: prepare path, clock, particle, or graph alternatives before factual detection
operations.distinguishability_partition: track whether alternatives remain coherent, marked, erased, or exchange-indistinguishable
operations.inheritance_update: apply phase, unitary network, marker, or graph-walk inheritance before readout
operations.readout_context: select the detector, output-port, clock-population, or graph-position context
operations.facticity_rule: facticize only the final path-summed or conditioned event
operations.stable_invariant: verify interference, visibility, I3, coincidence, or graph distribution invariants

qm_universal_patterns.claim_boundary: This pattern captures finite coherence/readout structure; it does not derive all optical or graph apparatus.

qm_universal_patterns.title: Phase/action anchor and periodic closure
qm_universal_patterns.mechanism: A declared action or charge anchor converts phase, flux, frequency, and threshold relations into finite readouts.

operations.event_packet: prepare transitions, emission alternatives, or flux-indexed paths
operations.distinguishability_partition: distinguish threshold crossing, line frequency, or phase-period class
operations.inheritance_update: inherit the declared action/charge anchor into phase, energy, frequency, or flux closure
operations.readout_context: read phase shift, flux period, emitted electron, or spectral line
operations.facticity_rule: facticize the threshold event or selected phase/frequency readout
operations.stable_invariant: verify E=hf, Delta phi=q Phi/hbar, Phi0=h/|q|, or shared-anchor consistency

qm_universal_patterns.claim_boundary: This pattern uses declared/calibrated anchors; it does not derive hbar, h, q, work functions, or atomic levels.

qm_universal_patterns.title: Finite update, reset, suppression, and survival
qm_universal_patterns.mechanism: A finite update transfers, resets, suppresses, or preserves weight depending on the intervening context sequence.

operations.event_packet: prepare two-state, barrier, or survival/transition alternatives
operations.distinguishability_partition: declare compatible, incompatible, forbidden, or repeated survival partitions
operations.inheritance_update: apply reset, drive, barrier suppression, or repeated readout update
operations.readout_context: read the final axis, population, reflection/transmission, or survival outcome
operations.facticity_rule: facticize the final context while preserving the declared update history
operations.stable_invariant: verify population transfer, context reset, nonzero suppressed transmission, or Zeno survival

qm_universal_patterns.claim_boundary: This pattern unifies finite update behavior; it does not derive microscopic drive, barrier, or continuous measurement dynamics.

qm_universal_patterns.title: Composite correlation and contextual obstruction
qm_universal_patterns.mechanism: Composite systems have context-indexed correlations that cannot be collapsed into one global factual assignment.

operations.event_packet: prepare joint, Bell-conditioned, product-context, projector, or temporal alternatives
operations.distinguishability_partition: declare which joint or compatible context is distinguishable per run
operations.inheritance_update: inherit correlation constraints through amplitude tables, angles, Bell facts, or temporal updates
operations.readout_context: read the selected joint, product, projector, Bell, or temporal context
operations.facticity_rule: reject any unregistered global fact table across incompatible contexts
operations.stable_invariant: verify CHSH, singlet angle, swapped correlation, parity obstruction, KS obstruction, or Leggett-Garg violation

qm_universal_patterns.claim_boundary: This pattern unifies finite contextual-correlation obstructions; it does not derive all operator algebras or physical analyzers.

qm_universal_patterns.title: Record stability, context transfer, and noncopyability
qm_universal_patterns.mechanism: Factual records can become stable, transferred, or excluded, but unknown context structure cannot be freely duplicated.

operations.event_packet: prepare system/environment records, source coincidences, input-target packets, or nonorthogonal states
operations.distinguishability_partition: track record recoverability, single-event exclusivity, branch correction, or overlap preservation
operations.inheritance_update: inherit records into environment, coincidence counts, Bell-branch transfer, or attempted copy closure
operations.readout_context: read residual coherence, g2(0), corrected target state, or overlap obstruction
operations.facticity_rule: mark stable records and single events while blocking unsupported universal copying
operations.stable_invariant: verify decoherence/recoverability loss, antibunching, target reconstruction, or no-cloning obstruction

qm_universal_patterns.claim_boundary: This pattern unifies finite record and transfer constraints; it does not derive sources, environments, or full physical networks.

qm_core_proof_obligations.title: Finite operational IDT core
qm_core_proof_obligations.open_gap: The current manifest has a stable operational vocabulary and 35 gate regressions, but the minimal axiom set is not yet proven independent or complete.
qm_core_proof_obligations.claim_boundary: Supported as an executable finite reconstruction program, not as a closed derivation of QM.

qm_core_proof_obligations.title: Context-indexed probability measure
qm_core_proof_obligations.open_gap: The verifier checks normalized finite readout tables, but the uniqueness of probability as the only admissible readout measure is not derived.
qm_core_proof_obligations.claim_boundary: The current layer verifies probability readouts; it does not yet derive the probability calculus from IDT primitives.

qm_core_proof_obligations.title: Distinguishability geometry
qm_core_proof_obligations.open_gap: Context-product exhaustion, purification/filtering, bounded correlations, noncomplex-Jordan separation, and generic-GPT closure are now executable for finite witnessed routes, but their extension into a universal carrier-selection theorem remains unresolved.
qm_core_proof_obligations.claim_boundary: This is the next derivation target; no Hilbert carrier is claimed from current primitives.

qm_core_proof_obligations.title: Hilbert-like carrier derivation
qm_core_proof_obligations.open_gap: The current gates use amplitudes and matrices as carriers; they do not derive complex Hilbert space as the forced carrier.
qm_core_proof_obligations.claim_boundary: Hilbert space remains an imported or reconstructed carrier until the geometry obstruction is closed.

qm_core_proof_obligations.title: Born rule derivation
qm_core_proof_obligations.open_gap: A finite quadratic readout route is now executable, but a universal Born theorem still depends on the carrier and measurement/facticity theorems.
qm_core_proof_obligations.claim_boundary: Born readouts have a finite executable route; full first-principles Born-rule derivation remains open.

qm_core_proof_obligations.title: Reversible inheritance and unitary symmetry
qm_core_proof_obligations.open_gap: The verifier checks finite unitary maps, but reversible inheritance has not yet been proven to force unitary or antiunitary structure.
qm_core_proof_obligations.claim_boundary: Unitary behavior is regression-supported; its derivation from IDT invariants is open.

qm_core_proof_obligations.title: Measurement, facticity, and recoverability
qm_core_proof_obligations.open_gap: Projection, partial readout, conditioning, and recoverability are checked as finite gates, but not yet derived as one measurement theorem.
qm_core_proof_obligations.claim_boundary: Finitary measurement behavior is executable; complete apparatus/facticity derivation is open.

qm_core_proof_obligations.title: Composite systems and tensor composition
qm_core_proof_obligations.open_gap: A finite tensor-composition route is now executable, but the full monoidal composition theorem remains open.
qm_core_proof_obligations.claim_boundary: Composite QM behavior has a finite executable route; full tensor composition remains a proof obligation.

qm_core_proof_obligations.title: Recompile the 35 QM gates from one finite core
qm_core_proof_obligations.open_gap: The current bench compiles pattern summaries, not every experiment as an instance of one proven finite core.
qm_core_proof_obligations.claim_boundary: The 35 gates are the regression target for the future core compiler, not evidence that the core proof is already closed.

qm_core_proof_obligations.title: Continuum and action-scale extension
qm_core_proof_obligations.open_gap: The finite core does not derive infinite-dimensional limits, Schrödinger dynamics, field modes, or first-principles hbar.
qm_core_proof_obligations.claim_boundary: This is outside the finite-core proof and must stay blocked while hbar_I and continuum construction remain open.
